PreCondition: gui_configuration.yaml is present at configs/gui/gui_configuration.yaml; PySide6 is installed; DISPLAY or virtual framebuffer available.
Test Name: App launches and window title matches YAML config
Steps: 1. Launch main.py (python main.py) ; 2. Wait for MainWindow to become visible ; 3. Read the window title bar text
Expected Result: Window title equals the value of window.title in gui_configuration.yaml (default: "Automate5 Industrial Control Hub").
Fail Conditions: Window does not appear; title text is empty, 'Automate5', or any value other than the one declared in gui_configuration.yaml.
Observations: main.py calls _load_window_cfg() and passes w.get('title') to window.setWindowTitle(). Falls back to 'Automate5 Industrial Control Hub' when the key is absent.
Next Action (if Fail): Check configs/gui/gui_configuration.yaml for window.title key; confirm _load_window_cfg() is not raising an exception silently.

PreCondition: gui_configuration.yaml present; PySide6 installed.
Test Name: Window dimensions match YAML width and height
Steps: 1. Launch main.py ; 2. Capture the window geometry after it is shown ; 3. Compare width and height against window.width / window.height in YAML
Expected Result: window.width() == YAML window.width (default 1920) and window.height() == YAML window.height (default 1080).
Fail Conditions: Window dimensions differ from YAML values by more than 2 pixels (excluding platform window chrome).
Observations: main.py calls window.resize(int(w.get('width', 1920)), int(w.get('height', 1080))). Off-by-one is acceptable due to OS DPI scaling; >2 px difference is a defect.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect _load_window_cfg() return value; check YAML integer types.

PreCondition: PySide6 installed; gui_configuration.yaml temporarily renamed or corrupted.
Test Name: App falls back to defaults when YAML config is missing or corrupt
Steps: 1. Rename configs/gui/gui_configuration.yaml to gui_configuration.yaml.bak ; 2. Launch main.py ; 3. Verify application starts without an unhandled exception ; 4. Verify window title and size use hard-coded defaults ; 5. Restore gui_configuration.yaml.bak
Expected Result: Application starts; window title is 'Automate5 Industrial Control Hub'; window is 1920x1080; no Python traceback is printed to stderr.
Fail Conditions: Application raises an unhandled exception at startup; or window does not appear.
Observations: _load_window_cfg() wraps yaml.safe_load in try/except and returns {} on any exception, so defaults apply silently.
Next Action (if Fail): Trace _load_window_cfg() exception path in main.py.

Next Action (if Fail): Verify _handle_reset loops all nodes/motors; check mock timer stop.

PreCondition: Base panel visible; system has not been started.
Test Name: KPI strip renders READY status on initial load
Steps: 1. Open Base panel ; 2. Read the KPI strip status field value

Fail Conditions: Status shows RUNNING or empty; totalRpm is non-zero before Play.
Observations: KpiStrip initial state is driven by base_transport_playing=false in QML.
Next Action (if Fail): Check initial value of base_transport_playing binding in QML.

PreCondition: System is RUNNING; mock_data.enabled: true.
Test Name: KPI totalRpm aggregates live mock RPM values across all motor cards
Steps: 1. Start the system (Play) ; 2. Wait 1000 ms for mock data to populate motor gauges ; 3. Sum the RPM values shown on all visible motor cards ; 4. Read totalRpm from the KPI strip
Expected Result: KPI totalRpm equals the sum of individual motor card RPM gauge values (within rounding tolerance of ±1 RPM).
Fail Conditions: KPI totalRpm does not match sum of motor card values; shows 0.
Observations: KpiStrip.totalRpm is computed from base_transport_playing and per-card values in QML, not the bridge directly.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect KpiStrip QML binding for totalRpm; verify motor card RPM properties are updated by bridge.base_update_motor().

PreCondition: System is RUNNING.

Expected Result: Uptime has increased by approximately 3 seconds.
Fail Conditions: Uptime stays at 0 or does not change after 3 seconds.
Observations: Uptime timer is a local QML timer driven by base_transport_playing; timer starts on Play and stops on Stop/Reset.
Next Action (if Fail): Check QML uptime timer binding to base_transport_playing.

PreCondition: Base panel visible; gui_configuration.yaml has base_panel.node_count: 2.

PreCondition: Analytics panel visible; currently in 'logs' or 'bench' mode.
Test Name: Selecting BOTH mode shows split view with both panes visible
Steps: 1. Click the 'BOTH' option in the mode picker row ; 2. Verify analytic_display_mode == 'both' ; 3. Verify both LogsAnalyticsPane and BenchmarkAnalyticsPane are visible
Expected Result: Both panes visible separated by draggable split handle; ratio ≈ 0.55.
Fail Conditions: One pane still hidden; ratio is not restored to default.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect SplitStack collapse/expand logic for 'both' mode.

PreCondition: Analytics panel in 'both' mode; split handle is visible.
Test Name: Dragging the split handle updates analytic_split_ratio within clamped range
Steps: 1. Drag split handle to a position corresponding to ratio ≈ 0.30 ; 2. Read analytic_split_ratio from bridge ; 3. Drag handle to a position below 0.15 (attempt out-of-range) ; 4. Verify analytic_split_ratio is clamped to 0.15 ; 5. Drag to above 0.85 (attempt out-of-range) ; 6. Verify analytic_split_ratio is clamped to 0.85
Expected Result: Ratio is updated to ≈ 0.30 on first drag; clamped to 0.15 at lower bound; clamped to 0.85 at upper bound.
Fail Conditions: Ratio goes outside [0.15, 0.85]; ratio not updated during drag.
Observations: AnalyticPanelBridge.analytic_set_split_ratio clamps value to [0.15, 0.85] before setting the property.
Next Action (if Fail): Verify clamping logic in analytic_set_split_ratio slot.

PreCondition: Analytics panel visible; no log file currently loaded.
Test Name: OPEN button in Logs pane opens FileDialog and accepted path triggers analytic_open_logs
Steps: 1. Click the 'OPEN' button in LogsAnalyticsPane header ; 2. In the FileDialog, select a valid .log or .jsonl file and accept ; 3. Verify analytic_bridge.analytic_log_path is updated to the selected path
Expected Result: FileDialog opens; on acceptance, analytic_open_logs(path) is invoked; analytic_log_path is set to the selected file path.
Fail Conditions: FileDialog does not open; path not passed to bridge after accept.
Observations: LogsAnalyticsPane opens a FileDialog; onAccepted strips the 'file:///' prefix and calls analytic_bridge.analytic_open_logs(path).
Next Action (if Fail): Check FileDialog onAccepted handler in LogsAnalyticsPane.qml.

PreCondition: A log file is loaded; log entries are visible.
Test Name: REFRESH button reloads the current log file and updates entries
Steps: 1. Note the current number of log entries ; 2. Append new entries to the log file on disk ; 3. Click the 'REFRESH' button in LogsAnalyticsPane ; 4. Verify the entry count has increased
Expected Result: analytic_refresh_logs() is called; log entries list is re-parsed; new entries from the appended data appear in the ListView.
Fail Conditions: Entry count unchanged after append + refresh; error banner appears.
Observations: analytic_refresh_logs_requested signal triggers presenter to reload file.
Next Action (if Fail): Trace analytic_refresh_logs → presenter load_log_file path.

PreCondition: A log file is loaded; log entries are visible.
Test Name: CLEAR button resets log data, path, summary, and error
Steps: 1. Click the 'CLEAR' button in LogsAnalyticsPane ; 2. Verify analytic_log_entries is empty ; 3. Verify analytic_log_path is empty string ; 4. Verify analytic_log_error is empty string
Expected Result: All log-related bridge properties reset to empty/null defaults; ListView shows 'No log file loaded' placeholder.
Fail Conditions: Entries or path remain non-empty; error string is not cleared.
Observations: AnalyticPanelPresenter._handle_clear_logs() calls set_logs_payload with empty payload which zeros all log bridge properties including error.
Next Action (if Fail): Verify set_logs_payload empties all fields in AnalyticPanelBridge.

PreCondition: Analytics panel visible; no log file loaded.
Test Name: Loading an invalid or missing log file path shows error banner
Steps: 1. Call analytic_bridge.analytic_open_logs('/nonexistent/path/run.jsonl') ; 2. Verify analytic_log_error is non-empty ; 3. Verify error banner (red rectangle) is visible in LogsAnalyticsPane
Expected Result: analytic_log_error contains a descriptive error message; error banner is visible; log entries list remains empty.
Fail Conditions: analytic_log_error is empty after an IO failure; banner not visible; application crashes on bad path.
Observations: load_log_file() in analytic_data.py returns {'error': ...} on IO/parse failure; presenter calls set_logs_payload; bridge exposes analytic_log_error.
Next Action (if Fail): Check error propagation from load_log_file → set_logs_payload → bridge.

PreCondition: A valid JSONL log file exists with at least 5 entries.
Test Name: Valid JSONL log file populates log ListView with subsystem-filtered entries
Steps: 1. Open the JSONL file via analytic_open_logs(path) ; 2. Verify analytic_log_error is empty ; 3. Verify analytic_log_entries has >= 5 items ; 4. Select a subsystem filter in the LogsAnalyticsPane filter dropdown ; 5. Verify the list shows only entries matching that subsystem
Expected Result: Entries visible in ListView; subsystem filter reduces displayed items to only matching entries.
Fail Conditions: Entries not displayed; filter shows all entries regardless of selection; analytic_log_error is non-empty for a valid file.
Observations: analytic_log_subsystems populates a filter dropdown in LogsAnalyticsPane.qml; filtering is applied in QML via a proxy model or JS filter.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect log entry binding and subsystem filter logic in LogsAnalyticsPane.qml.

PreCondition: Analytics panel visible; no benchmark file loaded.
Test Name: OPEN button in Benchmark pane opens CSV FileDialog and triggers analytic_open_bench
Steps: 1. Click the 'OPEN' button in BenchmarkAnalyticsPane header ; 2. Select a valid .csv benchmark file and accept ; 3. Verify analytic_bench_path is updated to the selected file path
Expected Result: FileDialog opens with CSV filter; on acceptance analytic_open_bench(path) is called; analytic_bench_path is set.
Fail Conditions: FileDialog does not open; path not passed to bridge; bench_path stays empty.
Observations: BenchmarkAnalyticsPane has a FileDialog with title 'Open benchmark CSV'; onAccepted strips file:/// prefix and calls analytic_bridge.analytic_open_bench(path).
Next Action (if Fail): Check FileDialog onAccepted handler in BenchmarkAnalyticsPane.qml.

PreCondition: A benchmark CSV is loaded; totals tiles show data.
Test Name: REFRESH button reloads the benchmark CSV and updates totals
Steps: 1. Note analytic_bench_totals.count ; 2. Append rows to the benchmark CSV on disk ; 3. Click 'REFRESH' in BenchmarkAnalyticsPane ; 4. Verify analytic_bench_totals.count has increased
Expected Result: analytic_refresh_bench() is called; benchmark data is re-parsed; totals (count, total_ms, avg_ms) reflect the appended rows.
Fail Conditions: Count unchanged after append + refresh; error banner appears on valid file.
Next Action (if Fail): Trace analytic_refresh_bench_requested → presenter load_benchmark_csv.

PreCondition: A benchmark CSV is loaded.
Test Name: CLEAR button resets benchmark data, path, totals, and error
Steps: 1. Click 'CLEAR' in BenchmarkAnalyticsPane ; 2. Verify analytic_bench_entries is empty ; 3. Verify analytic_bench_path is empty string ; 4. Verify analytic_bench_error is empty string ; 5. Verify totals tiles show 0 / '—'
Expected Result: All benchmark bridge properties reset to empty/zero defaults; pane shows 'Load a benchmark CSV to begin' placeholder.
Fail Conditions: Entries or path remain non-empty; totals not zeroed.
Observations: AnalyticPanelPresenter._handle_clear_bench() calls set_bench_payload with empty payload.
Next Action (if Fail): Verify set_bench_payload zeroes all bench bridge properties.

PreCondition: Analytics panel visible; no benchmark file loaded.
Test Name: Loading an invalid or missing CSV path shows error banner in Benchmark pane
Steps: 1. Call analytic_bridge.analytic_open_bench('/nonexistent/benchmark.csv') ; 2. Verify analytic_bench_error is non-empty ; 3. Verify error banner is visible in BenchmarkAnalyticsPane
Expected Result: analytic_bench_error contains an error message; red error banner visible; totals remain at zeros; no application crash.
Fail Conditions: Error banner not shown; bench_error is empty on failure; crash.
Observations: load_benchmark_csv() sets 'error' key in payload on failure; BenchmarkAnalyticsPane.qml error banner visibility bound to _error != ''.
Next Action (if Fail): Check error propagation from load_benchmark_csv → set_bench_payload → analytic_bench_error.

PreCondition: A valid benchmark CSV with at least 3 distinct phases is loaded.
Test Name: Benchmark totals KPI tiles display correct aggregates
Steps: 1. Load a known benchmark CSV; note expected count, total_ms, avg_ms, slowest_phase ; 2. Read analytic_bench_totals from bridge ; 3. Verify each tile value matches expected aggregate
Expected Result: MEASUREMENTS tile shows correct count; TOTAL tile shows correct total_ms; AVG tile shows correct avg_ms (±0.01 ms tolerance); SLOWEST PHASE tile shows the phase with highest avg_ms.
Fail Conditions: Any tile value is incorrect or zero for a non-empty dataset.
Observations: Totals are computed by load_benchmark_csv in analytic_data.py and stored in analytic_bench_totals; QML reads from bridge property.
Next Action (if Fail): Run load_benchmark_csv() directly on test CSV; compare returned totals against manually computed values.

PreCondition: Benchmark CSV loaded with at least 2 distinct phases.
Test Name: Per-phase bar chart renders one row per distinct phase
Steps: 1. Count the number of bar rows in the 'AVG BY PHASE' BarChart ; 2. Compare against the number of distinct phase values in analytic_bench_by_phase
Expected Result: BarChart row count equals len(analytic_bench_by_phase); each row label matches a phase name from the CSV.
Fail Conditions: Row count does not match; bar chart is empty with data present.
Observations: BenchmarkAnalyticsPane builds _phaseRows from analytic_bench_by_phase and passes to BarChart.model.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect _phaseRows computed property in BenchmarkAnalyticsPane.qml.

PreCondition: Benchmark CSV loaded with at least 2 distinct subsystems.
Test Name: Per-subsystem stacked bar chart renders one row per distinct subsystem
Steps: 1. Count the number of bar rows in the 'SUBSYSTEM ROLLUP' BarChart ; 2. Compare against the number of distinct subsystem values in analytic_bench_by_subsystem
Expected Result: Row count equals len(analytic_bench_by_subsystem); each row label matches a subsystem name; total_ms values are correct per row.
Fail Conditions: Row count mismatch; rollup chart empty with data loaded.
Observations: _subsystemRows computed from analytic_bench_by_subsystem; segments field used for stacked coloring.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect _subsystemRows computed property in BenchmarkAnalyticsPane.qml.

PreCondition: PySide6 installed; gui/components directory present; AppComponents qmldir registered under gui/components/AppComponents/.
Test Name: base_panel.qml compiles to QQmlComponent.Status.Ready without errors
Steps: 1. Create QQmlEngine; add gui/components to import path ; 2. Load gui/panels/base_design/qml/base_panel.qml via QQmlComponent ; 3. Poll status until not Loading (max 200 iterations) ; 4. Assert status == Ready; print any errors
Expected Result: Status is Ready; component.errors() is empty; AppComponents import resolves.
Fail Conditions: Status is Error; any import resolution or syntax error reported.
Observations: Corresponds to existing test_qml_loads in testcase_basePanel.py.
Next Action (if Fail): Print all QQmlComponent errors with file:line:column; fix missing import or syntax issue in base_panel.qml or AppComponents.

PreCondition: Same as TC-GUI-180.
Test Name: vla_panel.qml compiles to QQmlComponent.Status.Ready without errors
Steps: 1. Create QQmlEngine; add gui/components to import path ; 2. Load gui/panels/vla_design/qml/vla_panel.qml via QQmlComponent ; 3. Poll status until not Loading (max 200 iterations) ; 4. Assert status == Ready
Expected Result: Status is Ready; no QML errors.
Fail Conditions: Status is Error; import or syntax errors reported.
Observations: Corresponds to existing test_qml_loads in testcase_vlaPanel.py.
Next Action (if Fail): Print QQmlComponent errors; fix vla_panel.qml issues.

PreCondition: Same as TC-GUI-180.
Test Name: analytic_panel.qml compiles to QQmlComponent.Status.Ready without errors
Steps: 1. Create QQmlEngine; add gui/components to import path ; 2. Load gui/panels/analytic_design/qml/analytic_panel.qml via QQmlComponent ; 3. Poll status until not Loading (max 200 iterations) ; 4. Assert status == Ready
Expected Result: Status is Ready; no QML errors.
Fail Conditions: Status is Error; any import or syntax errors reported.

PreCondition: PySide6 installed; QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen or display available; heavy hardware controllers mocked.
Test Name: Real MainWindow bridge navigation synchronizes all panel active sections
Steps: 1. Instantiate MainWindow with Base, VLA, and Analytics panels ; 2. Emit each panel bridge navigation signal ; 3. Verify currentIndex and all active_section properties ; 4. Close the window and verify presenter shutdown
Expected Result: Full MainWindow navigation works without the lightweight stub stack.
Fail Conditions: Any bridge signal fails to switch page, active-section values diverge, or close leaves worker state active.
Observations: Adds deeper coverage beyond existing stub navigation tests.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect the referenced GUI bridge, presenter, QML, and test harness wiring.

PreCondition: BasePanelBridge and BasePanelPresenter can run headless; GestureController mocked.
Test Name: Base presenter motor handlers update state for transport and manual controls
Steps: 1. Create bridge and presenter ; 2. Emit motor start/stop/direction/setpoint/torque/reset/estop/mode signals ; 3. Observe presenter state and bridge updates ; 4. Verify reset and estop force safe zero state
Expected Result: Presenter consumes motor signals and updates state/logs consistently.
Fail Conditions: Signal ignored, state stale, reset/estop unsafe, or unsupported mode corrupts state.
Observations: Fills gap where motor cases mostly spy signal emission only.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect the referenced GUI bridge, presenter, QML, and test harness wiring.

PreCondition: VlaPanelBridge and VlaPanelPresenter can run headless; camera/Hololink dependencies mocked.
Test Name: VLA presenter prompt, transport, and camera branches are wired to bridge signals
Steps: 1. Create bridge and presenter ; 2. Emit prompt and transport signals ; 3. Emit mock configure and verify preview state ; 4. Emit real configure with mocked controller and verify arguments
Expected Result: VLA presenter handles prompt, transport, and camera configuration paths deterministically.
Fail Conditions: Prompt ignored, transport state wrong, mock configure fails, or controller receives wrong arguments.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect the referenced GUI bridge, presenter, QML, and test harness wiring.

PreCondition: Sample JSONL, log CSV, benchmark CSV, malformed files, and empty files available.
Test Name: Analytics loaders handle valid, empty, malformed, and missing log/benchmark files
Steps: 1. Load valid RuntimeLogger JSONL and benchmark CSV fixtures ; 2. Verify entries, summaries, phase aggregates, and subsystem aggregates ; 3. Load missing, empty, malformed, and wrong-header files ; 4. Verify deterministic error payloads without stale bridge data
Expected Result: Analytics parser returns correct data for valid files and deterministic errors for invalid files.
Fail Conditions: Malformed file crashes, summary math wrong, missing file not reported, or stale data remains.
Observations: Adds pure data coverage for analytic_data.py.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect the referenced GUI bridge, presenter, QML, and test harness wiring.

PreCondition: PySide6 installed; gui/components/AppComponents/qmldir present.
Test Name: High-risk AppComponents and analytics pane QML files compile independently
Steps: 1. Create QQmlEngine and add gui/components import path ; 2. Compile LogsAnalyticsPane and BenchmarkAnalyticsPane ; 3. Compile LeftRail, InspectorDock, CameraFeed, MotorCard, CameraHero, and NodeView ; 4. Print file:line:column errors for failures
Expected Result: Selected QML components resolve imports and reach Ready.
Fail Conditions: Any component fails to compile or AppComponents import cannot be resolved.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect the referenced GUI bridge, presenter, QML, and test harness wiring.

PreCondition: GUI loaded with double-camera mode and at least two camera options.
Test Name: InspectorDock rejects duplicate CAM1 and CAM2 selection in double mode
Steps: 1. Open Configure drawer or InspectorDock harness ; 2. Select double-camera mode ; 3. Attempt same source index for CAM1 and CAM2 ; 4. Trigger Configure ; 5. Observe warning and preview flags
Expected Result: Duplicate source selection is blocked and configure is not emitted with identical indexes.
Fail Conditions: Configure proceeds with duplicate indexes, no warning is shown, or preview state becomes inconsistent.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect the referenced GUI bridge, presenter, QML, and test harness wiring.

PreCondition: BasePanelPresenter uses injectable fake GestureController; no real camera required.
Test Name: Gesture overlay updates from fake gesture controller events
Steps: 1. Create Base bridge and presenter with fake GestureController ; 2. Enable gestures and emit motor, speed, stop, bbox, and frame-size events ; 3. Verify bridge gesture properties and notify signals ; 4. Reset tracking and verify overlay clears or unlocks
Expected Result: Gesture overlay state is testable without HIL camera by injecting deterministic events.
Fail Conditions: Bridge properties do not update, notify signals missing, or reset leaves stale overlay data.
Next Action (if Fail): Inspect the referenced GUI bridge, presenter, QML, and test harness wiring.
